name: Sierra de Mijas – circular from Alhaurín el Grande
name_original: Sierra de Mijas – circular desde Alhaurín el Grande
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande traverse a diverse landscape at the northern base of the Sierra de Mijas, extending into the fertile Guadalhorce Valley. The region features varied terrain, including paved roads through agricultural areas with olive and citrus groves, and more challenging climbs into mountainous zones. Whitewashed villages are interspersed throughout the area, offering scenic points of interest. This geographical setting provides a range of routes suitable for different cycling preferences. Best touring cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande The most popular touring cycling route is Sierra de Mijas – circular from Alhaurín el Grande, an 11.8 miles (19.0 km) trail that takes 1 hour 36 minutes to complete. This route offers views of the Sierra de Mijas mountains. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Alhaurín el Grande and Coín Loop, a moderate 16.0 miles (25.7 km) path. This route passes through agricultural land and the town of Coín. Local touring cyclists also love the Road Along the Sierra de Mijas – Castillo de La Mota loop from Alhaurín el Grande, a 22.8 miles (36.8 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 54 minutes. Touring cycling around Alhaurín El Grande is defined by the Sierra de Mijas mountains, the Guadalhorce Valley, and extensive olive and citrus groves. The network offers options for both gentle rides through agricultural areas and more challenging routes with significant elevation changes. The routes in Alhaurín El Grande are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Alhaurín El Grande's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Alhaurín El Grande?
A: There are over 35 touring cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences. These routes traverse diverse landscapes, from the fertile Guadalhorce Valley to the challenging Sierra de Mijas mountains.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: The terrain around Alhaurín El Grande is highly varied. You'll find gentle, paved roads winding through extensive olive and citrus groves in the Guadalhorce Valley, as well as more challenging climbs and descents in the mountainous Sierra de Mijas. Routes often pass through picturesque whitewashed villages and offer scenic views.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: Yes, there are a few easier touring cycling routes available. The Guadalhorce Valley, in particular, offers paved and less steep roads, ideal for those who prefer a more relaxed ride through agricultural land. While specific beginner routes are limited, many moderate routes can be enjoyed at a leisurely pace.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, there are 14 difficult touring cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31493881" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Road Along the Sierra de Mijas – Castillo de La Mota loop from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation changes and mountainous terrain. You can also venture towards El Torcal for steep climbs or the Lakes of Ardales for stunning scenery.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: Alhaurín El Grande enjoys a benign climate with mild winters and hot summers, offering sunshine for over two-thirds of the year. This makes it an ideal destination for touring cycling year-round. Spring and autumn provide particularly pleasant temperatures, while winters are mild enough for comfortable rides.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Alhaurín El Grande are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e980600425/sierra-de-mijas-circular-from-alhaurin-el-grande" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Mijas – circular from Alhaurín el Grande&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976850466/alhaurin-el-grande-and-coin-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alhaurín el Grande and Coín Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which passes through agricultural land and the town of Coín.

Q: What can I see along the touring cycling routes in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: Beyond the stunning natural landscapes of the Sierra de Mijas and the Guadalhorce Valley, you can explore charming whitewashed villages like Coín, Guaro, and Monda. Routes also pass through extensive olive and citrus groves. Nearby attractions include the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/90115" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Colomares Castle&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/295319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stupa of Enlightenment in Benalmádena&lt;/a&gt;, offering cultural and historical points of interest.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views. The Sierra de Mijas provides spectacular backdrops and coastal views, especially on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33833546" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cañada del Lobo Viewpoint – Trail from Monte Calamorro loop from Buenavista&lt;/a&gt;. You can also find panoramic vistas from various points in the mountains and overlooking the Guadalhorce Valley.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy the most about touring cycling in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. Touring cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, the mix of challenging climbs and gentle rides, and the opportunity to explore charming white villages and fertile agricultural lands. Over 400 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain.

Q: Can I find bike services like rentals or guided tours in Alhaurín El Grande?
A: Yes, bike rentals and both guided and self-guided tours are available in the Alhaurín El Grande area. Some services even offer delivery of bikes to your accommodation, making it convenient to start your cycling adventure.

Q: Are there any cultural attractions or historical sites accessible by bike?
A: Yes, the region is rich in history. You can cycle through or near traditional whitewashed Andalusian mountain villages such as Coín, Guaro, and Monda, which often feature historic churches and squares. Alhaurín El Grande itself has remnants of Roman columns and Arabic arches, and attractions like the Iglesia de Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación.

Q: What are some of the notable natural features I might encounter?
A: You'll encounter the dramatic Sierra de Mijas mountains, the lush Guadalhorce Valley with its extensive citrus and olive groves, and fragrant pine forests. The Guadalhorce River plains offer a refreshing environment, and birdwatchers might spot kestrels, black kites, and hoopoes.
tips: A city with a village soul. The most characteristic point is the church of Nuestra Señora de la Encarnación, which is located on the hill where there used to be the old castle and the mosque of the Muslim Alhaurín (in Andalusian times).
